exampleSentences.japanese: デパートは十時にあきますから、そのまえにあさごはんを食べましょう。
exampleSentences.english: The department store opens at 10, so let's eat breakfast before that.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はシーフードがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like seafood.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日本ではシーフードがとてもおいしいです。
exampleSentences.english: In Japan, seafood is very delicious.

exampleSentences.japanese: ほっかいどうはシーフードがゆうめいですから、たくさん食べたいです。
exampleSentences.english: Hokkaido is famous for seafood, so I want to eat a lot.

exampleSentences.japanese: こしょうをください。
exampleSentences.english: Pepper, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: このスープにこしょうをいれますか。
exampleSentences.english: Do you put pepper in this soup?

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はステーキにしおとこしょうをつかいますが、ソースはつかいません。
exampleSentences.english: I use salt and pepper on steak, but I don't use sauce.

english: bake, am going to bake, grill

exampleSentences.japanese: パンをやきます。
exampleSentences.english: I bake bread.

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日、クッキーをやきます。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going to bake cookies today.

exampleSentences.japanese: こんしゅうまつ、かぞくとにわでにくをやきましょう。
exampleSentences.english: This weekend, let's grill meat in the garden with the family.

exampleSentences.japanese: ほっかいどうのけしきをたのしみましょう！
exampleSentences.english: We should enjoy Hokkaido's views!

exampleSentences.japanese: りょこうをたのしみます。
exampleSentences.english: I will enjoy the trip.

exampleSentences.japanese: こんやのパーティーをたのしみましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Let's enjoy tonight's party.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来月、かぞくといっしょに京都でかんこうをたのしみます。
exampleSentences.english: Next month, I will enjoy sightseeing in Kyoto with my family.

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日は雨です。
exampleSentences.english: Today is rainy.

exampleSentences.japanese: 雨ですから、かさをもっていきます。
exampleSentences.english: It's raining, so I'll bring an umbrella.

exampleSentences.japanese: 明日、雨ですから、ピクニックはらいしゅうにしましょう。
exampleSentences.english: It will rain tomorrow, so let's have the picnic next week.

exampleSentences.japanese: ピクニックに行きます。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going on a picnic.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日曜日、こうえんでピクニックをしませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to have a picnic at the park on Sunday?

exampleSentences.japanese: てんきがいいですから、かぞくとこうえんでピクニックをたのしみましょう。
exampleSentences.english: The weather is nice, so let's enjoy a picnic at the park with the family.

exampleSentences.japanese: いつバーベキューをしましょうか。
exampleSentences.english: When should we have a barbecue?

exampleSentences.japanese: 一日休みます。
exampleSentences.english: I will rest for one day.

exampleSentences.japanese: 五月一日にコンサートがあります。
exampleSentences.english: There is a concert on May 1st.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は一日にコーヒーを三つ飲みますが、のみすぎですか。
exampleSentences.english: I drink three cups of coffee a day, but is that too much?

exampleSentences.japanese: ええと、五月一日がいいです。
exampleSentences.english: Um, May first is good.

exampleSentences.japanese: どこがいいですか。
exampleSentences.english: Where is good?

exampleSentences.japanese: 山のちかくの川がいいです。
exampleSentences.english: The river near the mountains is good.

exampleSentences.japanese: キャンプがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like camping.

exampleSentences.japanese: なつやすみにキャンプに行きたいです。
exampleSentences.english: I want to go camping during summer vacation.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来月、ともだちと山でキャンプをして、バーベキューをたのしみます。
exampleSentences.english: Next month, I will camp in the mountains with friends and enjoy a barbecue.

exampleSentences.japanese: ハイキングに行きます。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going hiking.

exampleSentences.japanese: しゅうまつ、山でハイキングをしませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to go hiking in the mountains this weekend?

exampleSentences.japanese: あきはすずしいですから、ハイキングにいいきせつです。
exampleSentences.english: Fall is cool, so it's a good season for hiking.

exampleSentences.japanese: これは私のバックパックです。
exampleSentences.english: This is my backpack.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は新しいバックパックを買いたいです。
exampleSentences.english: I want to buy a new backpack.

exampleSentences.japanese: りょこうのまえに、大きいバックパックをデパートで買いましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Before the trip, let's buy a big backpack at the department store.

exampleSentences.japanese: ほっかいどうに行きませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to go to Hokkaido?

exampleSentences.japanese: ほっかいどうのけしきをたのしみましょう！
exampleSentences.english: We should enjoy Hokkaido's views!

exampleSentences.japanese: ねこはすみにいます。
exampleSentences.english: The cat is in the corner.

exampleSentences.japanese: カフェはあのたてもののすみにあります。
exampleSentences.english: The cafe is in the corner of that building.

exampleSentences.japanese: へやのすみに大きいほんだなをおきましたから、本がたくさんはいります。
exampleSentences.english: I put a big bookshelf in the corner of the room, so it can hold many books.

exampleSentences.japanese: このふくはカラフルなです。
exampleSentences.english: These clothes are colorful.

exampleSentences.japanese: はらじゅくにはカラフルなふくがたくさんあります。
exampleSentences.english: There are many colorful clothes in Harajuku.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすめはカラフルなドレスがだいすきですから、たんじょうびにプレゼントしましょう。
exampleSentences.english: My daughter loves colorful dresses, so let's give her one as a birthday present.

exampleSentences.japanese: このカフェにハムスターがよんひきいます。
exampleSentences.english: There are four hamsters in this cafe.

exampleSentences.japanese: カメレオンを見ました。
exampleSentences.english: I saw a chameleon.

exampleSentences.japanese: どうぶつえんでカラフルなカメレオンを見ませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to see a colorful chameleon at the zoo?

exampleSentences.japanese: こどもはカメレオンがだいすきですから、きのうどうぶつえんでたくさんしゃしんをとりました。
exampleSentences.english: The children love chameleons, so they took many photos at the zoo yesterday.

exampleSentences.japanese: ねこはなんびきいますか。
exampleSentences.english: How many cats are there?

exampleSentences.japanese: いぬはなんびきいますか。三びきです。
exampleSentences.english: How many dogs are there? There are three.

exampleSentences.japanese: あなたのうちにはどうぶつがなんびきいますか。ねこがにひきといぬがいっぴきいます。
exampleSentences.english: How many animals are in your house? There are two cats and one dog.

exampleSentences.japanese: このへやにくろいねこがさんびきいます。
exampleSentences.english: There are three black cats in this room.

exampleSentences.japanese: このへやにどんなどうぶつがいますか。
exampleSentences.english: What kind of animals are there in this room?

exampleSentences.japanese: このへやにくろいねこがさんびきいます。
exampleSentences.english: There are three black cats in this room.

exampleSentences.japanese: へやは三階です。
exampleSentences.english: The room is on the third floor.

exampleSentences.japanese: 口をあけてください。
exampleSentences.english: Please open your mouth.

exampleSentences.japanese: いしゃは私の口を見ました。
exampleSentences.english: The doctor looked at my mouth.

exampleSentences.japanese: からいものを食べましたから、口がいたいです。水をください。
exampleSentences.english: I ate spicy food, so my mouth hurts. Water, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: トマトがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like tomatoes.

exampleSentences.japanese: スーパーであかいトマトを買いました。
exampleSentences.english: I bought red tomatoes at the supermarket.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のにわでトマトをそだてていますから、まいにちサラダに入れます。
exampleSentences.english: I grow tomatoes in my garden, so I put them in salads every day.

exampleSentences.japanese: そのいぬのみみはながいですね。
exampleSentences.english: That dog's ears are long, aren't they?

exampleSentences.japanese: そのいぬのみみはながいですね。
exampleSentences.english: That dog's ears are long, aren't they?

exampleSentences.japanese: このカフェにハムスターがよんひきいます。
exampleSentences.english: There are four hamsters in this cafe.

exampleSentences.japanese: このかばんはちゃいろです。
exampleSentences.english: This bag is brown.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のいぬはちゃいろとしろです。
exampleSentences.english: My dog is brown and white.

exampleSentences.japanese: あきには木のはがちゃいろとオレンジになりますから、とてもきれいです。
exampleSentences.english: In fall, the tree leaves turn brown and orange, so it's very beautiful.

exampleSentences.japanese: パンケーキを食べます。
exampleSentences.english: I eat pancakes.

exampleSentences.japanese: 日曜日のあさ、いつもパンケーキをつくります。
exampleSentences.english: On Sunday mornings, I always make pancakes.

exampleSentences.japanese: このカフェのパンケーキはふわふわであまいですから、とてもにんきです。
exampleSentences.english: This cafe's pancakes are fluffy and sweet, so they're very popular.

exampleSentences.japanese: カプチーノをください。
exampleSentences.english: A cappuccino, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はまいあさカプチーノを飲みます。
exampleSentences.english: I drink a cappuccino every morning.

exampleSentences.japanese: このカフェのカプチーノはおいしいですから、ともだちとよく来ます。
exampleSentences.english: This cafe's cappuccino is delicious, so I often come here with friends.

exampleSentences.japanese: にんきのねこカフェに行きませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to go to a popular cat cafe?

exampleSentences.japanese: このへやにどんなどうぶつがいますか。
exampleSentences.english: What kind of animals are there in this room?

exampleSentences.japanese: チェスができますか。
exampleSentences.english: Can you play chess?

exampleSentences.japanese: 父とチェスをするのがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like playing chess with my father.

exampleSentences.japanese: チェスはむずかしいですが、あにはとてもじょうずです。
exampleSentences.english: Chess is difficult, but my older brother is very good at it.

exampleSentences.japanese: ボランティアサークルはたのしいサークルです。
exampleSentences.english: The volunteer club is a fun club.

exampleSentences.japanese: ボランティアサークルはたのしいサークルです。
exampleSentences.english: The volunteer club is a fun club.

exampleSentences.japanese: ボードゲームがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like board games.

exampleSentences.japanese: しゅうまつ、かぞくとボードゲームをしませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Would you like to play board games with the family this weekend?

exampleSentences.japanese: 雨の日はそとにでかけませんから、うちでボードゲームをたのしみます。
exampleSentences.english: On rainy days, we don't go outside, so we enjoy board games at home.

exampleSentences.japanese: キャンパスのちかくにすんでいます。
exampleSentences.english: I live near campus.

exampleSentences.japanese: キャンパスに何がありますか。
exampleSentences.english: What's there on campus?

exampleSentences.japanese: キャンパスに大きい木があります。
exampleSentences.english: There's a big tree on campus.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はりょうに住んでいます。
exampleSentences.english: I live in a dorm.

exampleSentences.japanese: 大学のりょうはキャンパスのちかくにあります。
exampleSentences.english: The university dorm is near the campus.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のりょうはせまいですが、ルームメイトはとてもしんせつです。
exampleSentences.english: My dorm is small, but my roommate is very kind.

exampleSentences.japanese: 駅はどこですか。
exampleSentences.english: Where is the station?

exampleSentences.japanese: 駅からうちまで十分かかります。
exampleSentences.english: It takes 10 minutes from the station to my house.

exampleSentences.japanese: 東京駅はとても大きいですから、はじめての人はちずをもっていってください。
exampleSentences.english: Tokyo Station is very big, so first-time visitors should bring a map.

exampleSentences.japanese: タガログごを話しますか。
exampleSentences.english: Do you speak Tagalog?

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のともだちはフィリピンからきましたから、タガログごができます。
exampleSentences.english: My friend is from the Philippines, so they can speak Tagalog.

exampleSentences.japanese: タガログごはフィリピンのげんごですが、えいごも話します。
exampleSentences.english: Tagalog is the language of the Philippines, but they also speak English.

exampleSentences.japanese: ドイツごをべんきょうします。
exampleSentences.english: I study German.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はドイツごがすこしできます。
exampleSentences.english: I can speak German a little.

exampleSentences.japanese: 来年、ドイツにりょこうしたいですから、ドイツごをべんきょうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I want to travel to Germany next year, so I'm studying German.

exampleSentences.japanese: 何げんごを話しますか。
exampleSentences.english: What languages do you speak?

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は三つのげんごができます。
exampleSentences.english: I can speak three languages.

exampleSentences.japanese: 新しいげんごをべんきょうするのはむずかしいですが、たのしいです。
exampleSentences.english: Studying a new language is difficult, but it's fun.

exampleSentences.japanese: せんこうはすうがくです。
exampleSentences.english: My major is math.

exampleSentences.japanese: ビジネスをべんきょうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I'm studying business.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私のせんこうはビジネスです。
exampleSentences.english: My major is business.

exampleSentences.japanese: 大学でビジネスをべんきょうしてから、大きいかいしゃではたらきたいです。
exampleSentences.english: After studying business at university, I want to work at a big company.

exampleSentences.japanese: アンドリューさんのせんこうは何ですか。
exampleSentences.english: What is your major, Andrew?

exampleSentences.japanese: せんこうはすうがくです。
exampleSentences.english: My major is math.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は四年生です。
exampleSentences.english: I'm a senior.

exampleSentences.japanese: あねは大学の四年生です。
exampleSentences.english: My older sister is a senior in college.

exampleSentences.japanese: 四年生ですから、来年そつぎょうしてから、しごとをさがします。
exampleSentences.english: I'm a senior, so after graduating next year, I'll look for a job.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすこは三年生です。
exampleSentences.english: My son is a third grader.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は大学の三年生で、ビジネスをべんきょうしています。
exampleSentences.english: I'm a junior in college, studying business.

exampleSentences.japanese: 三年生になりましたから、せんこうのじゅぎょうがおおくなりました。
exampleSentences.english: Since I became a junior, I have more major classes.

exampleSentences.japanese: サムさんはがくせいです。
exampleSentences.english: Sam is a student.

exampleSentences.japanese: サムさんはアメリカからきました。
exampleSentences.english: Sam came from America.

exampleSentences.japanese: サムさんは日本ごをべんきょうしていますから、いっしょにカフェで話しませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Sam is studying Japanese, so would you like to chat together at a cafe?

exampleSentences.japanese: 何かいですか。
exampleSentences.english: What floor?

exampleSentences.japanese: レストランは何かいにありますか。
exampleSentences.english: What floor is the restaurant on?

exampleSentences.japanese: このデパートは何かいありますか。八かいあります。
exampleSentences.english: How many floors does this department store have? It has eight floors.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は二年生です。
exampleSentences.english: I'm a sophomore.

exampleSentences.japanese: おとうとは今、こうこうの二年生です。
exampleSentences.english: My younger brother is now a sophomore in high school.

exampleSentences.japanese: 二年生になってから、新しいともだちがたくさんできました。
exampleSentences.english: Since becoming a sophomore, I've made many new friends.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私は一年生です。
exampleSentences.english: I'm a freshman.

exampleSentences.japanese: むすめは大学の一年生になりました。
exampleSentences.english: My daughter became a college freshman.

exampleSentences.japanese: 一年生ですから、キャンパスはまだよくわかりませんが、まいにちたのしいです。
exampleSentences.english: Since I'm a freshman, I still don't know the campus well, but every day is fun.

exampleSentences.japanese: かいさんは何年生ですか。
exampleSentences.english: What school year are you, Kai?

exampleSentences.japanese: みなみぐちで会いましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Let's meet at the south exit.

exampleSentences.japanese: みなみぐちをでて、左にまがってください。
exampleSentences.english: Exit through the south exit and turn left.

exampleSentences.japanese: 駅のみなみぐちのまえにバスていがありますから、そこで会いましょう。
exampleSentences.english: There's a bus stop in front of the station's south exit, so let's meet there.

exampleSentences.japanese: 飲み物は何がいいですか。
exampleSentences.english: What would you like to drink?

exampleSentences.japanese: つめたい飲み物をください。
exampleSentences.english: Please give me a cold drink.

exampleSentences.japanese: パーティーのために、いろいろな飲み物をスーパーで買いましょう。
exampleSentences.english: Let's buy various drinks at the supermarket for the party.

exampleSentences.japanese: タクシーをよびます。
exampleSentences.english: I'll call a taxi.

exampleSentences.japanese: ともだちをパーティーによびませんか。
exampleSentences.english: Shall we invite friends to the party?

exampleSentences.japanese: 雨がふっていますから、タクシーをよびましょうか。
exampleSentences.english: It's raining, so shall I call a taxi?

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日はゆっくりします。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going to relax today.

exampleSentences.japanese: しゅうまつは何をしますか。うちでゆっくりします。
exampleSentences.english: What will you do on the weekend? I'll relax at home.

exampleSentences.japanese: しごとがいそがしかったですから、今日はうちでゆっくりしたいです。
exampleSentences.english: Work was busy, so I want to relax at home today.

exampleSentences.japanese: ツアーに行きます。
exampleSentences.english: I'm going on a tour.

exampleSentences.japanese: 京都のツアーはいくらですか。
exampleSentences.english: How much is the Kyoto tour?

exampleSentences.japanese: はじめて日本に来ましたから、かんこうツアーによやくしたいです。
exampleSentences.english: Since it's my first time in Japan, I want to book a sightseeing tour.

exampleSentences.japanese: インフォメーションはどこですか。
exampleSentences.english: Where is the information desk?

exampleSentences.japanese: インフォメーションでちずをもらいました。
exampleSentences.english: I got a map at the information desk.

exampleSentences.japanese: わからないことがありますから、インフォメーションできいてみましょう。
exampleSentences.english: There's something I don't understand, so let's try asking at the information desk.

exampleSentences.japanese: これは八百円です。
exampleSentences.english: This is 800 yen.

exampleSentences.japanese: このコーヒーは八百円です。やすいですね。
exampleSentences.english: This coffee is 800 yen. That's cheap.

exampleSentences.japanese: 千円ではらいましたから、おつりは八百円です。
exampleSentences.english: I paid with 1000 yen, so the change is 800 yen.

english: pay, going to pay, will pay

exampleSentences.japanese: 私がはらいます。
exampleSentences.english: I'll pay.

exampleSentences.japanese: クレジットカードではらいますか。
exampleSentences.english: Will you pay by credit card?

exampleSentences.japanese: 今日は私のたんじょうびですから、父がばんごはんをはらいます。
exampleSentences.english: Today is my birthday, so my father will pay for dinner.

exampleSentences.japanese: エスカレーターはどこですか。
exampleSentences.english: Where is the escalator?

exampleSentences.japanese: エスカレーターで三階に行ってください。
exampleSentences.english: Please go to the third floor by escalator.

exampleSentences.japanese: エレベーターはこんでいますから、エスカレーターをつかいましょう。
exampleSentences.english: The elevator is crowded, so let's use the escalator.

exampleSentences.japanese: きたぐちでタクシーをよびましょう。
exampleSentences.english: We should call a taxi at the north exit.

exampleSentences.japanese: 三ばんホームです。
exampleSentences.english: It's platform number three.

exampleSentences.japanese: でんしゃは三ばんホームからでます。
exampleSentences.english: The train leaves from platform number three.

exampleSentences.japanese: しぶや行きのでんしゃは三ばんホームですから、かいだんで上がってください。
exampleSentences.english: The train to Shibuya is on platform number three, so please go up the stairs.

english: switching, going to transfer, are switching

exampleSentences.japanese: ここでのりかえます。
exampleSentences.english: I'll transfer here.

exampleSentences.japanese: しぶやでバスにのりかえてください。
exampleSentences.english: Please transfer to the bus at Shibuya.

exampleSentences.japanese: 東京駅でしんかんせんにのりかえますから、時間にちゅういしてください。
exampleSentences.english: I'll transfer to the bullet train at Tokyo Station, so please mind the time.

exampleSentences.japanese: かいそくにのります。
exampleSentences.english: I'll take the rapid-service train.

exampleSentences.japanese: かいそくのほうがはやいです。
exampleSentences.english: The rapid-service train is faster.

exampleSentences.japanese: かいそくでんしゃは駅にあまりとまりませんから、はやくつきます。
exampleSentences.english: The rapid-service train doesn't stop at many stations, so it arrives quickly.

exampleSentences.japanese: 二ばんホームです。
exampleSentences.english: It's platform number two.

exampleSentences.japanese: でんしゃは二ばんホームからでます。
exampleSentences.english: The train leaves from platform number two.

exampleSentences.japanese: つぎのでんしゃは二ばんホームですから、エスカレーターでおりてください。
exampleSentences.english: The next train is on platform number two, so please go down by escalator.

exampleSentences.japanese: オレンジがすきです。
exampleSentences.english: I like oranges.

exampleSentences.japanese: あさ、オレンジジュースを飲みます。

exampleSentences.japanese: フライドポテトがすきです。

exampleSentences.english: The children love french fries, so they always order them at restaurants.

exampleSentences.english: Today's job training is from eleven o'clock.

exampleSentences.english: Please print this document on the printer before the meeting.

exampleSentences.japanese: さむいですから、コートをきます。

exampleSentences.english: There's a big tree on campus.

exampleSentences.japanese: ちかくに安いショッピングセンターがあります。
exampleSentences.english: There is a cheap shopping center nearby.

exampleSentences.english: Mr. Smith is good at Japanese, so he always speaks in Japanese.

exampleSentences.english: Mr. Yamada is very kind, so he often teaches me new work.

exampleSentences.english: I'm studying Spanish.

exampleSentences.english: I can speak a little Spanish.

exampleSentences.english: I want to travel to Spain next year, so I'm studying Spanish now.

exampleSentences.english: The Japanese classroom is on the second floor.

exampleSentences.japanese: このきょうしつはひろくてあかるいですから、べんきょうしやすいです。

exampleSentences.english: I don't really like salty food.

exampleSentences.english: This ramen is a bit salty, so I drink a lot of water.

exampleSentences.japanese: コーラをください。
exampleSentences.english: Cola, please.

exampleSentences.japanese: 私はコーラがすきですが、あまり飲みません。
exampleSentences.english: I like cola, but I don't drink it much.

exampleSentences.english: I only use salt and pepper on steak, but I don't use sauce.

exampleSentences.english: This sauce is spicy, but it's very delicious, so please try using it.

exampleSentences.english: Sushi uses raw fish.

exampleSentences.english: Japanese cuisine has many dishes using raw fish, so freshness is important.

exampleSentences.japanese: とうふがすきです。

exampleSentences.english: I put tofu in miso soup.

exampleSentences.japanese: とうふは体にいいですから、私はまいにち食べています。
exampleSentences.english: Tofu is good for the body, so I eat it every day.
